name: plaid-fintech
description: Expert patterns for Plaid API integration including Link token flows, transactions sync, identity verification, Auth for ACH, balance checks, webhook handling, and fintech compliance best practices. Use when: plaid, bank account linking, bank connection, ach, account aggregation.
capabilities: Guide Link token creation and public_token exchange workflows
Explain transactions/sync endpoint for incremental updates
Provide webhook handling patterns for real-time updates
Document Item error handling and update mode flows
Share anti-patterns to avoid like plain text token storage
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analyzer flagged 5 patterns as potential security issues, but all are false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' matches are triggered by the word 'verification' in documentation text describing Plaid API features. The 'system reconnaissance' matches are triggered by 'link_token' which is a legitimate Plaid API concept for OAuth-style bank connections. This skill contains only documentation with no executable code, making it safe for publication.
valueStatement: Building fintech apps requires secure Plaid API integration patterns. This skill provides proven approaches for Link token flows, transaction sync, webhook handling, and compliance requirements.
userTitle: Integrate Plaid API for Banking Data and Payments
limitations: Documentation only - does not execute API calls directly
Requires valid Plaid API credentials from user
Does not replace official Plaid documentation
Patterns may need updates as Plaid API evolves

useCases.title: Fintech Startup Onboarding
useCases.description: A developer building a personal finance app needs to connect user bank accounts securely. Use this skill to implement the Link token flow and handle authentication errors gracefully.

useCases.title: Payment Application Integration
useCases.description: An e-commerce platform wants to enable ACH payments. Use this skill to implement Auth verification and handle ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED errors during payment setup.

useCases.title: Transaction Analysis Tool
useCases.description: A budgeting app needs efficient transaction syncing without redundant API calls. Use this skill to implement /transactions/sync and webhook-based updates instead of polling.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Link Token Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to implement Plaid Link in my app. Show me the flow for creating a link_token server-side, presenting it to users, and exchanging the public_token for an access_token.

promptTemplates.title: Transaction Sync Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me implement efficient transaction fetching using /transactions/sync instead of /transactions/get. Show how to handle the cursor for incremental updates and detect when full refresh is needed.

promptTemplates.title: Webhook Handler Design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a webhook handler for Plaid webhooks. Focus on ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED and PENDING_DISCONNECT events. Show how to notify users proactively and guide them through update mode.

promptTemplates.title: Error Handling Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a comprehensive error handling strategy for Plaid API integration. Cover Item errors, API errors, and user-facing messages. Include retry logic and when to prompt users to re-link accounts.

bestPractices: Store access_tokens encrypted at rest using platform key management services
Use webhooks for real-time updates instead of polling to reduce API calls
Handle PENDING_DISCONNECT webhooks proactively to minimize user friction
antiPatterns: Storing access tokens in plain text or client-side storage
Polling /transactions/get repeatedly instead of using /transactions/sync with webhooks
Ignoring Item errors which can lead to stale data and broken user experiences

faq.question: Do Link tokens expire?
faq.answer: Yes, Link tokens are short-lived (typically 30 minutes) and single-use. Create a new token each time a user needs to link an account or update credentials.

faq.question: Do access tokens expire?
faq.answer: Access tokens do not expire by default. However, they may become invalid if users change their bank credentials. Listen for ITEM_LOGIN_REQUIRED errors to detect when re-authentication is needed.

faq.question: What is the difference between /transactions/get and /transactions/sync?
faq.answer: Use /transactions/sync for incremental updates with cursor-based pagination. It returns only new, modified, or removed transactions since your last sync. /transactions/get returns all transactions and is less efficient for regular updates.

faq.question: How do I handle webhook security?
faq.answer: Verify webhook signatures using Plaid's verification key. Validate the webhook payload structure and ensure your endpoint is HTTPS-only. Handle webhooks asynchronously to avoid timeout issues.

faq.question: What products should I enable for Link tokens?
faq.answer: Only enable products you actively use. Common combinations: auth (for ACH verification), transactions (for transaction history), identity (for account holder verification). Each enabled product affects pricing.

faq.question: How do I test Plaid integration without real bank accounts?
faq.answer: Use Plaid Sandbox environment with test credentials. Sandbox provides deterministic test accounts with controllable behaviors like pending disconnects, item errors, and specific transaction patterns.
